Let me compile the final report: ## Comprehensive Report: Onboarding Flow and Channel Configuration Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a detailed understanding of how the onboarding flow works and how channels are displayed and configured: ### 1. **Onboarding Files Structure** **Main Onboarding Implementation Files:** - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/templates/onboarding.html` - Minimal HTML template that loads the JS app - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-app.js` - Entry point that initializes i18n and mounts the onboarding view - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/js/onboarding-view.js` - **Main implementation file** (55K+ tokens) containing all onboarding step components - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/js/page-onboarding.js` - Simple router integration file **Localization Files:** - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/en/onboarding.js` - English strings - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/fr/onboarding.js` - French strings - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/js/locales/zh/onboarding.js` - Chinese (Simplified) strings ### 2. **Onboarding Steps Sequence** The onboarding follows a multi-step wizard pattern (from `onboarding-view.js`, lines 2-6): 1. **Auth** (conditional) - Security setup with passkey/password 2. **Identity** - User and agent identity configuration 3. **Provider** - LLM provider setup 4. **Voice** (conditional) - Speech-to-text and text-to-speech configuration 5. **Remote Access** - Tailscale Funnel/ngrok setup 6. **Channel** - Messaging channel connection (our focus) 7. **Summary** - Configuration overview and final status ### 3. **Channel Step Implementation** **Location:** `onboarding-view.js`, lines 3659-3714 **Phase State Machine:** The channel step has three phases: - `"select"` - User selects which channel type to connect - `"form"` - User fills out the specific channel configuration form - `"success"` - Confirmation that channel was connected successfully **Channel Type Selector Component:** ```javascript function ChannelTypeSelector({ onSelect, offered }) { var channelOptions = [ ["telegram", "icon-telegram", "Telegram"], ["whatsapp", "icon-whatsapp", "WhatsApp"], ["msteams", "icon-msteams", "Microsoft Teams"], ["discord", "icon-discord", "Discord"], ["matrix", "icon-matrix", "Matrix"], ["nostr", "icon-nostr", "Nostr"], ].filter(([type]) => offered.has(type)); return html`
${channelOptions.map(([type, iconClass, label]) => html``) }
`; } ``` **CSS Classes for Channel Cards:** - `.backend-card` - Base card styling from `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/ui/input.css` (lines 1536-1559) - Has hover, selected, and disabled states - Border transitions on interaction - Flexbox column layout with padding ### 4. **Currently Included Channel Forms in Onboarding** **Available Forms:** (lines 3701-3706) 1. **TelegramForm** - Full implementation starting at line 2726 2. **WhatsAppForm** - Full implementation starting at line 3328 3. **TeamsForm** (Microsoft Teams) - Full implementation starting at line 2821 4. **DiscordForm** - Full implementation starting at line 3003 5. **MatrixForm** - Full implementation starting at line 3110 6. **NostrForm** - Full implementation starting at line 3545 ### 5. **CRITICAL: Slack is Missing from Onboarding** **Key Finding:** Slack channel is **NOT** included in the onboarding wizard forms, even though it's listed in the default offered channels. **Evidence:** - Line 3660: Default offered channels include `"slack"` in the fallback list: `["telegram", "whatsapp", "discord", "slack", "matrix"]` - Lines 3701-3706: Conditional rendering for forms shows Telegram, WhatsApp, Teams, Discord, Matrix, and Nostr - **NO SLACK FORM** - Line 3515: `channelDisplayLabel()` function does handle "slack" type for display purposes **Slack Configuration Location:** Slack configuration is **only available in the Channels Settings page** (`page-channels.js`), not in onboarding: - Lines 474-480: "Connect Slack" button in ConnectButtons component - Lines 1236-1319: AddSlackModal implementation with full configuration form ### 6. **How channels_offered is Determined** **Flow:** 1. **Rust Backend Configuration** (`crates/config/src/schema/runtime.rs`, lines 286-296): ```rust fn default_channels_offered() -> Vec { vec![ "telegram".into(), "whatsapp".into(), "msteams".into(), "discord".into(), "slack".into(), "matrix".into(), "nostr".into(), ] } ``` 2. **Runtime Resolution** (`crates/web/src/templates.rs`): - Line 408: `resolve_channels_offered()` function checks if `moltis.toml` config file exists - Line 510-524: If config found, loads `channels.offered` from file; otherwise uses default - Lines 409-413: Creates `ChannelDescriptor` objects from the resolved list for UI hints 3. **Frontend Injection** (`crates/web/src/templates.rs`, line 73): - `channels_offered: Vec` is part of the `GonData` struct - Passed to JavaScript via `gon_json` (server-injected data) in templates - Line 3660 of `onboarding-view.js`: `var offeredList = getGon("channels_offered") || [...]` **Configuration via moltis.toml:** Users can customize which channels are offered in onboarding by adding to their config: ```toml [channels] offered = ["telegram", "slack", "matrix"] # Only these 3 channels shown ``` ### 7. **Channel Selection Pattern: Cards vs Dropdowns** The implementation uses **clickable selection cards** instead of dropdowns (CLAUDE.md requirement): - `.backend-card` styling provides visual feedback on hover and selection - Responsive grid: 2 columns on mobile (`grid-cols-2`), 3 columns on desktop (`md:grid-cols-3`) - Icons displayed above label with flexbox centering - Minimum height of 120px with 8px vertical padding ensures touch-friendly size ### 8. **Storage and Channel Configuration** **From `channel-utils.js` imports in onboarding-view.js (lines 12-30):** - `addChannel()` - REST endpoint to add a channel - `fetchChannelStatus()` - RPC call to get current channel status - `validateChannelFields()` - Validates channel configuration - `parseChannelConfigPatch()` - Parses advanced JSON config patch **Storage Location:** - Channel data stored in `~/.moltis/moltis.db` (SQLite, not `moltis.toml`) - Per CLAUDE.md: "web UI channel settings live in `data_dir()/moltis.db`, not `moltis.toml`" ### 9. **Channel Card Display in Summary** After onboarding completes, the Summary Step (lines 4102-4129) displays connected channels: - Shows channel type (e.g., "slack", "discord") - Shows account ID/name - Shows status (connected/disconnected/error) with color indicators: - Green dot for "connected" - Yellow dot for "disconnected" - Red dot for "error" ### 10. **Localization Structure** **English locale structure** (`locales/en/onboarding.js`): - Lines 4-13: Step labels (including `channel: "Channel"`) - Lines 161-184: Channel-specific strings (only Telegram covered, not comprehensive) - Lines 198-199: Generic channel labels for summary display **Key localization keys:** - `onboarding:steps.channel` - Step name - `onboarding:channel.connectTelegram` - Telegram-specific instructions - `onboarding:summary.channelsLabel` - "Channels" label in summary - `onboarding:summary.noChannelsConfigured` - Fallback when no channels connected ### 11. **CSS and Styling** **Files:** - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/ui/input.css` - Tailwind CSS input with `.backend-card` class - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/css/components.css` - Channel form styles - `/Users/penso/tmp/molt/moltis/crates/web/src/assets/css/base.css` - Base styles **Key channel-specific classes:** - `.channel-form` - Flex column container with 12px gap - `.channel-card` - Info card background with border and padding - `.channel-help` - Flex container for help text items - `.channel-input` - Text input styling - `.channel-select` - Dropdown styling - `.channel-error` - Error display (hidden by default) - `.backend-card` - Selection card for type choice (2-3 column grid) ### 12. **Remote Access Note** Line 2590-2591 of onboarding-view.js notes: > "Public endpoints are optional for most channels, but Microsoft Teams needs one. Enable Tailscale Funnel, ngrok, or both before connecting team channels." This is shown in the Remote Access step before the Channel step. ### Summary The onboarding flow displays **6 channel types via selection cards** (Telegram, WhatsApp, Teams, Discord, Matrix, Nostr), with each having a dedicated form component. **Slack is notably absent from onboarding** but available only in the Settings Channels page. The channels offered are configurable via `moltis.toml` and are injected into the frontend via server-side template data (`GonData`). The UI uses Preact/HTM for component rendering, Tailwind CSS for styling, and follows the "clickable cards" pattern specified in CLAUDE.md rather than dropdown menus.

   - Conditional App Token field (for Socket Mode)
   - Conditional Signing Secret field (for Events API)
   - DM Policy
   - User allowlist
   - Advanced config patch
   - Setup instructions matching the channels page
